name: mongodb-atlas
description: Inspect MongoDB Atlas projects, clusters, and database users via the Atlas Administration API

envVars.name: MONGODB_ATLAS_CLIENT_ID
envVars.description: Atlas service account client ID (e.g. mdb_sa_id_...)

envVars.name: MONGODB_ATLAS_CLIENT_SECRET
envVars.description: Atlas service account client secret (e.g. mdb_sa_sk_...)

tools.name: List Projects
tools.description: List Atlas projects (groups) the service account can access

tools.name: List Clusters
tools.description: List clusters in an Atlas project

groupId.description: Atlas project (group) ID — a 24-character hex string from List Projects

historicalSummary.omitted: replication specs, connection strings, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get Cluster
tools.description: Get details of a cluster, including state and connection strings

tools.name: List Database Users
tools.description: List database users configured in an Atlas project

historicalSummary.omitted: scopes, labels, and provider-specific payload fields

prompts.title: Cluster overview
prompts.prompt: List my MongoDB Atlas projects and the clusters in each, with their state and MongoDB version.

prompts.title: Find paused clusters
prompts.prompt: Check my MongoDB Atlas clusters and tell me which ones are paused or not in the IDLE state.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: MongoDB Atlas API Setup

steps.title: Sign in to Atlas
steps.description: Go to https://cloud.mongodb.com and sign in (a free M0 cluster is enough for testing).

steps.title: Create a service account
steps.description: In Organization Access Manager > Applications > Service Accounts, create a service account with at least Organization Read Only (or Project Read Only on specific projects). Copy the client ID and client secret — the secret is shown only once.

steps.title: Add your IP to the API access list
steps.description: Service account access tokens can only be USED from IPs on the service account's API access list. Add your machine's IP (or 0.0.0.0/0 for testing only).

steps.title: Set environment variables
steps.description: Add MONGODB_ATLAS_CLIENT_ID and MONGODB_ATLAS_CLIENT_SECRET to your .env. Tokens are minted automatically from https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/oauth/token via the OAuth2 client_credentials grant (credentials sent via HTTP Basic).

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run List Projects, then List Clusters with a project's ID.

notes: Legacy Atlas programmatic API keys use HTTP Digest auth, which this connector does NOT support — use a service account (OAuth2 client_credentials) instead; MongoDB recommends service accounts over API keys

Every request must send a versioned Accept header; tools default to application/vnd.atlas.2025-03-12+json (the latest stable resource version). Atlas returns 406 if the header is missing or names a nonexistent version
Access tokens expire after 1 hour and are refreshed by re-running the client_credentials grant
